title: Regular Verb Conjugation — -át, -ít, -et/-ět Classes
description: Learn to conjugate regular Czech verbs in present tense across three conjugation classes

exercises.prompt: What is the 'já' (1st person singular) form of 'dělat' (to do)?

data.question_en: What is the 'já' (1st person singular) form of 'dělat'?
data.explanation: The -át class adds -ám for 1st person singular: dělat → dělám.

exercises.prompt: What is the 'ty' (2nd person singular) form of 'mluvit' (to speak)?

data.question_en: What is the 'ty' (2nd person singular) form of 'mluvit'?
data.explanation: The -ít class adds -íš for 2nd person singular: mluvit → mluvíš.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the correct form of 'bydlet' (to live) for 'já'.

data.explanation: 'Bydlet' conjugates with -í endings like the -ít class. 1st person singular: bydlím. 'Já bydlím v Praze.' = 'I live in Prague.'

exercises.prompt: Fill in the correct form of 'mluvit' (to speak) for 'ona' (she).

data.explanation: The -ít class has the same ending for 3rd person singular and plural: mluví. 'Ona mluví česky.' = 'She speaks Czech.'

data.grammar_note: 'Znát' is an -át class verb. 1st person singular: znám. The pronoun 'ho' (him) comes after the verb.

accepted_answers: I am waiting for the bus.
data.grammar_note: 'Čekat' is an -át class verb. 1st person singular: čekám. 'Na' + accusative = 'for'.

exercises.answer_key: I am waiting for the bus.

exercises.prompt: Arrange the words to form: 'We speak Czech.'

data.explanation: 'My mluvíme česky.' = 'We speak Czech.' The -ít class 1st person plural ends in -íme.

exercises.prompt: Which verb belongs to the -ít conjugation class?

data.question_en: Which verb belongs to the -ít conjugation class?
data.explanation: 'Mluvit' ends in -ít and follows the -ím, -íš, -í pattern. 'Dělat', 'znát', and 'čekat' all end in -át.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the correct form of 'rozumět' (to understand) for 'ty' (you).

data.explanation: 'Rozumět' belongs to the -et/-ět class. 2nd person singular: rozumíš. 'Ty rozumíš česky?' = 'Do you understand Czech?'

exercises.prompt: Listen and write what you hear.

data.note: 'Rozumím' is the 1st person singular of 'rozumět' (-et/-ět class).

exercises.prompt: What is the 1st person singular ('já') form of 'číst' (to read)?

data.question_en: What is the 1st person singular ('já') form of 'číst'?
data.explanation: 'Číst' is a special verb in the -et/-ět group. The 1st person singular is 'čtu'. Other forms: čteš, čte, čteme, čtete, čtou.
